response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional seniority and software engineering discipline across diverse technical domains, the resume shows significant gaps in the core technical requirements. C++ experience is limited to 2000–2001 (20+ years ago) with modern work heavily concentrated on TypeScript/Node.js. More critically, there is no evidence of expertise in industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443 standards, secure development lifecycle practices, or IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA)—all foundational to this role. The candidate's strengths lie in general architecture and system design, but the industrial automation domain expertise required is absent.
strengths: Extensive senior-level experience (30+ years) with proven track record architecting systems, mentoring teams, and applying software craftsmanship principles including TDD and CI/CD
Demonstrated expertise in event-driven architecture and distributed systems through GISCollective platform and high-throughput microservices work
Strong foundation in low-latency performance optimization and async I/O patterns from Sociomantic/Dunnhumby work handling millions of requests per second
concerns: C++ experience is severely outdated (last substantive work in 2000–2001); modern career dominated by TypeScript/Node.js with no recent C++ projects or frameworks like STL
No demonstrated expertise in industrial cybersecurity, secure development lifecycle (SDL), IEC 62443 standards, or compliance frameworks central to the role
Missing critical IoT/industrial protocols and expertise: no mention of MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, IEC 104, DNP 3.0, OPC UA, or experience with industrial automation systems

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap (20-year absence from language)
Industrial cybersecurity and IEC 62443 domain expertise absent
Senior-level architecture and system design experience
